notes: Two vibe lifetimes back-to-back — variant 1 (clean exit via /exit) → variant 2 (SIGKILL mid-idle). RE-SPEC (record backflow): the templated 3rd variant (SIGKILL mid-turn) was dropped — vibe batch-writes messages.jsonl only on turn completion (save_interaction), so a mid-turn SIGKILL leaves no session_<ts>_<shortid> transcript to key on (unlike claudecode's streaming partial writes). vibe is a PRESESSION adapter: each variant is born as a proc-<PID> pre-session in ready (vN_session_birth), then the real session_<ts>_<hash> dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id (vN_handoff new_session, never enters waiting), then drives ready→working (vN_turn_start). OBSERVED: each turn is immediately followed (~2s later) by a benign working→ready activity blip ('force ready→working on first activity' as vibe rewrites its sidecar), so v2_turn_done does NOT assert 'never working post-turn'; it asserts the session settles to ready and process_exited fires from ready. Session-end signal is process_exited (transcripts persist on disk). BACKFLOW: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so they are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens and matches kiro-cli (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false).
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Session end (re-spec: 2 recordable variants for vibe)

text: Variant 1: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Variant 1: real session_<ts>_<hash> dir reconciles (different session_id); never enters waiting through end of its lifetime
Variant 1: first user prompt drives the real session ready → working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready
Variant 1: clean exit (/exit) — process_exited fires on the real session AFTER state returned to ready (proves 'State is ready at exit')
Variant 2: new proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: real session dir reconciles (different session_id); never enters waiting over its lifetime
Variant 2: user prompt drives the real session ready → working

Variant 2: turn completes back to ready; a benign post-turn activity blip (working→ready) may follow as vibe rewrites its sidecar, but the session never enters waiting and settles in ready before the SIGKILL
Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires on the real session while it is in ready
